title: Acidanthera longicollis
intro: Acidanthera longicollis, also known as the 'River Lily', is a plant species native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents present in Acidanthera longicollis include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory conditions and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Acidanthera longicollis is classified under the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This flowering plant is also categorized as a genus within the Iridaceae family.
